hypothesis: In MADE-like closed-loop materials discovery campaigns, a Bayesian multi-fidelity active-learning policy that explicitly maximizes the expected information gain about the convex hull of stability (allocating queries adaptively across cheap MLIP evaluations, higher-fidelity DFT calculations, and experimental validations) will discover more novel thermodynamically stable compounds within a fixed oracle budget than policies that optimize only immediate stability or rely on a single fidelity.
